NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Examples

GitHub Repo Inspection Bundle

A portable-evidence bundle for read-only repo inspection, policy-constrained events, and offline receipt verification.

The GitHub repo inspection example is the first concrete portable-evidence bundle for Northroot primitives.

It models a read-only repository inspection where an agent checks required files and leaves behind structured evidence. The example uses ordinary files first: JSON for actor, obligation, policy, receipt, and manifest objects; JSONL for the append-only event log.

Evidence Bundle

FileRole
actor.jsonDeclares the repo inspector agent and its authority scope.
obligation.jsonDefines the inspection objective, constraints, and required receipts.
policy.jsonAllows read access and denies write or merge actions.
events.jsonlRecords accepted obligation, tool invocation, and receipt generation events.
receipt.jsonBinds the evidence hashes to a passed inspection claim.
manifest.jsonLists the bundle files and their canonical hashes.
cargo run -p northroot -- validate examples/github-repo-inspection/obligation.json
cargo run -p northroot -- hash examples/github-repo-inspection/events.jsonl
cargo run -p northroot -- verify examples/github-repo-inspection/receipt.json \
  --base-dir examples/github-repo-inspection \
  --json

What The Example Proves

  • A completed work item can be described without a hosted workflow engine.
  • Mutation remains explicitly denied by policy.
  • Cost attribution can be recorded even when the cost is zero.
  • Verification can happen from local evidence files with no live service.
  • The JSONL records can be replayed into a temporary .nrj journal and verified.

Implementation Notes

Runnable fixtures stay in examples/github-repo-inspection/. Generated .nrj journals remain temporary derived artifacts; the checked-in bundle stays readable as ordinary text.

Operational Lessons

A useful example should leave behind evidence that can be inspected after it leaves the runtime that produced it.

Source

northroot/examples/github-repo-inspection/

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
